mission.title: distribution: Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes multi-arch manifests
mission.description: Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes multi-arch manifests. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current distribution deployment
steps.description:
Verify your distribution version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
helm list -n distribution 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working distribution installation.

steps.title: Review distribution configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution
```
Hello!
We are currently experimenting with using multi-arch images in our infrastructure, as we have a mix of IBM Power and regular AMD64-based machines, and are currently using different tags/repositories to distinguish between the architectures,

steps.title: Apply the fix for Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes…
steps.description:
Updates the Manifest and ManifestBuilder to distinguish between blob and manifest references. Uses this new interface to fix the garbage collection logic to do a proper transitive closure on references so that untagged sub manifests referenced by (tagged) manifest lists do not get removed even if
```yaml
# Remove old registry, clean slate
docker rm -f registry
docker system prune --all --volumes -f

# Start registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry

# Pull AMD64-image
docker pull "docker"
docker tag "docker" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"

# ARM-image
docker pull "ppc64le/docker"
docker tag "ppc64le/docker" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"

#Create and push manifest for multi-arch ubuntu
docker manifest create --insecure "localhost:5000/docker:latest" "localhost:50
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Registry garbage-collect --delete-untagged removes…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Updates the Manifest and ManifestBuilder to distinguish between blob and manifest references. Uses this new interface to fix the garbage collection logic to do a proper transitive closure on references so that untagged sub manifests referenced by (tagged) manifest lists do not get removed even if remove-untagged is given to the garbage collector.
codeSnippets:
# Remove old registry, clean slate
docker rm -f registry
docker system prune --all --volumes -f

# Start registry
docker run -d -p 5000:5000 --name registry registry

# Pull AMD64-image
docker pull "docker"
docker tag "docker" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest"

# ARM-image
docker pull "ppc64le/docker"
docker tag "ppc64le/docker" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker push "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"

#Create and push manifest for multi-arch ubuntu
docker manifest create --insecure "localhost:5000/docker:latest" "localhost:5000/amd64/docker:latest" "localhost:5000/ppc64le/docker:latest"
docker manifest push -p "localhost:5000/docker:latest"

# Clear local cache
docker system prune --all -f

# Garbage-collect registry
dock
